finding: GOAL-FRAME GEOMETRY BUG: block_to_goal_distance/block_at_goal build the goal via combine_frame_transforms(robot.data.root_pos_w,...), but the RM robot articulation root is at world z=-0.805 (fixed chassis root below the lift pole). cmd z=0.04 -> goal lands at z=-0.765, ~0.8m UNDERGROUND. dist_block_goal~1.0m for all envs, success geometrically impossible. success_rate=0 is forced by geometry, not reward.

Verified fix: compute goal in env-origin frame (goal_w = env.scene.env_origins + des_pos_b, identity rotation) instead of robot root frame. Then goal lands at table height (~z=0.04) and ~2.6cm from block at reset; cmd ranges (-0.3,-0.1)x(-0.3,-0.1) put goals 0-0.2m from block reset region -> reachable by pushing.

iter0: success_rate=0.000, total_return=3.606. reaching=3.29 (EE ~5cm from block, sustained, block not knocked away), block_to_goal_coarse=0.32, fine=0.0, success=0.0. Term ladder kept from proven library base push-block-franka. Reward design is fine; blocker is the goal-frame geometry.
Secondary: success/reward use 3D distance, but task desc says 'block-to-goal XY distance < 5cm'. After goal-frame fix, make block_at_goal (and optionally tracking) xy-only to match the 2D-goal spec.

iter1: GEOMETRY FIX WORKED. success_rate 0.000->0.098, total_return 3.6->67.5. Goal frame changed robot-root->env-origin, block_at_goal now XY-only. coarse tracking 0.32->65.4 (block reaches ~12cm avg from goal), fine(std=0.05) only 2.12, reaching collapsed to 0.027 (EE leaves block after shove - normal for push).

Last-centimeters problem: block plateaus ~10-12cm from goal. coarse kernel std=0.3 nearly flat there, fine kernel std=0.05 too sharp to give gradient until <5cm -> gradient dead-band in 5-15cm range. Only 9.8% reach XY<5cm. Next: add mid-band tracking term std~0.1-0.15 to bridge coarse and fine.

iter2: added mid_band(std=0.12,w=8) + fine std 0.05->0.08. success FLAT 0.098->0.100, total 67.5->84.7. All 3 kernels imply block still plateaus ~10-11cm from goal - same as iter1. Added gradient raised reward but did NOT move converged block position. Reward-gradient shaping is EXHAUSTED.

BOTTLENECK IS CONTROL not reward: reaching collapsed to ~0 (EE ends 31cm from block). Policy learned open-loop SWAT - one shove then EE retracts, block coasts to stop ~10cm short. No closed-loop fine pushing. Fix must incentivize SUSTAINED EE-block CONTACT: raise reaching weight (1->3-5) and/or contact-gate the fine tracking term (only credit when EE near block). Keep coarse attractor ungated.

iter3 CONVERGED: success_rate 0.100->0.507 (>=0.5 threshold). Change: reaching weight 1->4 + contact-gate mid/fine tracking (x (1-tanh(d_ee_block/0.05))). reaching 0.003->0.631 (arm stays engaged), coarse 65->85.3 (block ~12cm->~6cm from goal). Gated mid/fine collapsed to ~0 - exploit removed. Genuine closed-loop pushing confirmed in video.

KEY LESSON for push tasks: when success plateaus despite reward-gradient shaping and reaching collapses to ~0, the policy is doing open-loop swatting. Fix = sustained-contact incentive: raise reaching weight AND contact-gate the precision tracking terms so they cannot be farmed without touching the object. This broke a 0.10->0.51 plateau in one iter.
